criteria.label: Run `python -m cProfile -s cumulative script.py` and read the top 10 lines.
criteria.example: `python -m cProfile -s cumulative pipeline.py | head -20` — identify the slow path.

criteria.label: Save profiles with `-o profile.out` and analyse later with `pstats`.
Distinguish cumtime vs tottime and use both views on every investigation.
criteria.example: Cumulative top-10 reveals the path; tottime top-10 reveals where the work actually is.

criteria.label: Profile a focused region with the `cProfile.Profile()` context manager or a `@profiled` decorator.
Visualize non-trivial profiles with snakeviz; communicate findings with screenshots.
criteria.example: `snakeviz profile.out` — share a screenshot in the PR description showing the before/after icicles.

criteria.label: Pair cProfile with `py-spy` (production), `line_profiler` (line-level), `tracemalloc/memray` (memory) when needed.
criteria.example: cProfile → suspects a slow function → `line_profiler` narrows to one line → fix; for prod, `py-spy` for sampling.
